reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a Salesforce Release & DevOps specialist with 7 years of experience in Copado, Flosum, Jenkins, and CI/CD pipeline management — none of which is relevant to this embedded software engineering role. The JD demands C programming, MISRA coding standards, AUTOSAR, RTOS, and RADAR system knowledge. Not a single must-have skill is met or even partially evidenced anywhere in the resume. The candidate operates at Technology Lead level in the Salesforce ecosystem, which is a completely different engineering discipline. Additionally, the candidate is based in Bengaluru, not Chennai, adding a location mismatch for an in-office role. Do not proceed — this is a domain mismatch, not a seniority or gap issue.
strengths: SALESFORCE DEVOPS DEPTH: Leads end-to-end CI/CD automation using Copado, Flosum, and Jenkins across multi-org Salesforce environments for major clients including Apple and Philip Morris International — high-signal enterprise delivery experience, though entirely irrelevant to this role.

CERTIFICATION PORTFOLIO: Holds 11+ Salesforce and DevOps certifications including Copado AI Certified, Flosum Expert, and Salesforce Agentforce Specialist — rare breadth for any Salesforce professional, but zero value for an embedded C/AUTOSAR/RADAR position.

COMMUNITY RECOGNITION: Selected as a Copado Mentor and holds Agentblazer Innovator credentials — indicates peer-recognised expertise within the Salesforce ecosystem, though this signal is irrelevant to the hiring decision here.
gaps: ZERO EMBEDDED SYSTEMS EXPERIENCE: The role mandates C programming, MISRA compliance, AUTOSAR, and RTOS — none of these appear anywhere in the candidate's 7-year career history. This is a total domain mismatch, not a skill gap that can be bridged.

NO RADAR OR ADAS KNOWLEDGE: Both RADAR system knowledge and ADAS familiarity are listed as required or advantageous — the candidate has no automotive, embedded, or safety-critical systems background whatsoever, making her unqualified for even the baseline requirements.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated in the resume; no penalty applied.
Candidate is based in Bengaluru; JD requires in-office presence in Chennai.
Candidate has 7 years total experience, well above the minimum threshold of 2 years (half of 4 required).
JD gender preference is 'Any'; no penalty applied.
No college exclusion or preference specified in the JD; no adjustment applied.

availability_reason: Current role at Infosys is listed as August 2025 to Present with no notice period stated; availability cannot be determined.
alternative_role_fit: Senior Salesforce DevOps Engineer / Salesforce Release Manager
rare_assets: Copado Mentor Program selection — peer-recognised mentorship role awarded by Copado, rare among practitioners.
Dual Flosum certification at Expert level combined with Copado AI certification — uncommon combination in Salesforce DevOps market.
Agentforce Specialist certification — cutting-edge Salesforce AI credential very few practitioners currently hold.
self_reported_unverified: 'Rise Insta Award' for exceptional performance — no context on criteria, scope, or how many recipients receive this award.
Claims of 'automating CI/CD pipelines' at Apple — no metrics on deployment frequency, failure rate reduction, or scale of automation.
Leading Salesforce DevOps for PMI — leadership scope and team size are not quantified.
interview_flags: Confirm whether candidate has any exposure to embedded C programming, even academic or personal projects.
Ask why candidate applied to an embedded/automotive software role given their pure Salesforce background.
Probe actual hands-on coding depth vs. configuration/admin work in Salesforce roles — ratio of code-to-config is unclear.
Clarify team size managed at Infosys as 'Technology Lead' — is this a people management role or an individual contributor title?
Ask about relocation willingness to Chennai given current Bengaluru base for this in-office role.
